concept: Building Dashboards with Generative Pages (GPT-5 Powered)
description: Creating interactive dashboard screens with KPIs, charts, animations, and interactive filtering using Generative Pages powered by GPT-5 in model-driven Power Apps.

gpt5_enhancements.context_understanding: Enhanced comprehension of complex dashboard requirements

dashboard_components.description: Key metric displays at top of dashboard
dashboard_components.prompt_example: Show key KPIs at the top, total tickets, open tickets, close tickets

dashboard_components.use_case: Ticket trends in the last 6 months

technical_implementation.description: JavaScript library for dynamic interactive data visualizations
technical_implementation.architecture: React-based page generated by app agent

animation_capabilities.description: Bars grow from bottom up when appearing
animation_capabilities.prompt: Bar charts should grow from the bottom up when they appear

animation_capabilities.description: Lines draw themselves from left to right
animation_capabilities.prompt: Lines should draw themselves across the chart from left to right

animation_capabilities.description: Interactive hover states on chart elements

brand_customization.description: Apply company brand standards via natural language
brand_customization.prompt_example: Create a professional dashboard theme using a dark background with primary color #XXXXXX, secondary accent #XXXXXX...

theme_toggle.prompt: Add a simple toggle to switch between dark mode, light mode

interactive_filtering.description: Chart selections filter the data table
interactive_filtering.prompt: When the user clicks or selects a part of a chart, it should filter the tickets table to only show the tickets that match that chart selection

prompting_best_practices: Be specific about KPIs and their placement
Specify chart types explicitly (bar, line, pie)
Include color codes for brand theming
Describe animations in detail (direction, speed, style)

key_quotes.quote: Gen pages is now powered with GPD5. Better quality, improved performance.
It uses the D3 library which is designed for producing dynamic interactive data visualizations. It heavily uses SVGs.
You can get very specific with the kind of animations and transitions you would like to have.

limitations: Future updates will enable code editing
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of D3/SVG (helpful but not required)
